At the moment the Conservators are so unpopular that many of their local representatives, especially if outgoing mayors, set the election campaign taking the distance from the party: posters and adverts do not mention it, instead giving evidence to the names of candidates, who in the campaign in their statements have emphasized several times personal and political differences regarding their leadership. In general, the Conservative Party is likely to lose half of the almost one thousand seats currently occupied in municipal councils. The outgoing mayor of London Sadiq Khan with his wife Saadiya Ahmed (AP Photo/Kin Cheung) He voted altogether in England, for 10 mayors and for 37 police and crime commissioners (PCCs), i.e. for public security supervisors linked to police districts, England and Wales. There are no elections in Scotland and Northern Ireland. In the College of Blackpool South (England) there will be additional elections for the parliamentary seat left vacant by the resignation of Conservative Scott Benton, involved: Labourers are widely favored. In three metropolitan areas you will vote for the mayor () for the first time. Since 2007 a law allows local governments to establish public bodies () that gather metropolitan areas or areas similar to our provinces. In these areas the mayor is elected directly by the citizens, while previously municipal councils chose it. City councils continue to choose in cities not considered metropolitan areas. East Midlands, North East, York and North Yorkshire will vote for the first time, but the most important elections are those held in London (Greater London), the Birmingham (West Midlands) and the north-eastern Tees Valley (which includes Darlington, Hartlepool, Middlesbrough, Cleveland and Stockton-on-Tees). The outgoing mayor of Tees Valley Ben Houchen at the vote (Photo by Ian Forsyth/Getty Images) In London the outgoing mayor Sadiq Khan, Labour, should make sure without too much trouble a third term. Andy Street and Ben Houchen are the outgoing mayors of West Midlands and Tees Valley: both conservatives, both come from two consecutive mandates and both are very important within the party. Today their re-election is in doubt especially for the Conservatory crisis, more than for a decline in personal popularity. For this Street from Sunak («I support the party, but he and I have strayed on a number of issues»), and in the ballots also the colors of the background are different from the classic blue of the Tories (the Conservators). Houchen, who in 2021 won with 73 percent of the votes, also eliminated some references to the party from flyers and spots and said that many voters assured him that they will vote despite losing confidence in the Conservatories. The outgoing mayor of the West Midlands Andy Street (Photo by Darren Staples/Getty Images)The previous local elections of the areas that will vote today date back to 2021, when the Conservative Party then led by Boris Johnson was at its peak in popularity. He obtained 985 seats in the municipal councils: Many analysts believe that falling below 500 would be a worrying signal even compared to the low current expectations. The vote will obviously be conditioned by local peculiarities and it is not always easy or correct to draw conclusions on the national trend based on local results, but the problems considered central by voters are more or less the same almost everywhere: the increase in the cost of life and loans, economic stagnation and the crisis of public health are some of the issues on which the British government gave partial or ineffective answers, according to most voters. Sunak, on the other hand, points a lot to the transfer of asylum seekers in Rwanda, who had a very troubled path and on whose application however there are still doubts. At national level for Labour seems to be the positioning of the party against the ongoing war in Gaza: the left of the party and the electorate of Islamic religion believe that Secretary Keir Starmer was not quite resolute and clear in asking for immediate ceasefire and that his approach was too moderate to Israel.
en_subtitle: That is why before today's vote many Conservative candidates had distanced themselves from Prime Minister Rishi Sunak of their own party: polls predict a general victory of Labour

title: La giunta militare che governa il Myanmar ha vietato agli uomini di andare a lavorare all’estero
content: La giunta militare che governa il Myanmar dal 2021 ha sospeso il rilascio dei permessi per gli uomini che vogliono lavorare all’estero. Il ministero del Lavoro ha fatto sapere che la sospensione è temporanea ed è stata decisa per «avere più tempo per verificare le procedure» per la partenza degli uomini, ma non delle donne, che sono in età per fare il servizio militare.A inizio febbraio la giunta aveva  il servizio militare obbligatorio per uomini e donne, applicando una legge del 2010 fino a quel momento mai attuata che prevedeva la leva obbligatoria. La leva è ora obbligatoria per gli uomini di età compresa tra i 18 e i 35 anni e per le donne tra i 18 e i 27, per un massimo di due anni. Per chi ha una formazione medica il limite di età è 45 anni e il massimo del periodo obbligatorio sale a tre.Il ministero del Lavoro ha detto che la sospensione non si applicherà agli uomini a cui è già stato concesso il permesso di partire. Un rappresentante del ministero ne ha parlato con il sito indipendente a cui non ha voluto dire se questa decisione sia legata al gran numero di uomini che hanno cercato di andare a lavorare all’estero da quando è entrato in vigore il servizio militare obbligatorio.
en_title: The military junta governing Myanmar has banned men from working abroad
en_content: The military junta governing Myanmar since 2021 has suspended the release of permits for men who want to work abroad. The Ministry of Labour has announced that the suspension is temporary and has been decided to "have more time to check procedures" for the departure of men, but not women, who are at age to do military service. At the beginning of February the junta had the compulsory military service for men and women, applying a 2010 law until that moment never implemented that provided for the compulsory lever. The lever is now mandatory for men aged between 18 and 35 and for women between 18 and 27, for a maximum of two years. For those who have a medical education the age limit is 45 years and the maximum of the compulsory period rises to three. The Ministry of Labour said that the suspension will not apply to men who have already been granted permission to leave. A ministry representative spoke of it with the independent site that he did not want to say if this decision is linked to the large number of men who have tried to go to work abroad since the compulsory military service came into force.

title: Almeno 29 persone sono morte a causa di gravi inondazioni nel sud del Brasile
content: Almeno 29 persone  e 60 risultano disperse in seguito alle gravi inondazioni degli ultimi giorni nello stato del Rio Grande do Sul, nel sud del Brasile. La situazione peggiore si è verificata nella zone intorno al fiume Guaiba, vicino a Porto Alegre, dove la forte pioggia ha provocato esondazioni in varie aree. Le autorità locali hanno detto che quasi 3.400 persone di oltre 100 comuni sono state trasferite in alloggi di emergenza. In alcune aree, le inondazioni sono così gravi che intere città sono rimaste completamente isolate, con strade e ponti distrutti. Giovedì inoltre una diga per una piccola centrale idroelettrica è collassata, lasciando oltre 300mila persone senza elettricità.
en_title: At least 29 people died due to serious floods in southern Brazil
en_content: At least 29 people and 60 are dispersed following the serious floods of the last days in the state of Rio Grande do Sul, in southern Brazil. The worst situation occurred in the area around the Guaiba River, near Porto Alegre, where the heavy rain caused floods in various areas. Local authorities said that nearly 3,400 people of over 100 municipalities were transferred to emergency accommodation. In some areas, floods are so serious that entire cities have remained completely isolated, with roads and destroyed bridges. Thursday also a dam for a small hydroelectric power plant is collapsed, leaving over 300 thousand people without electricity.

title: Il fondatore ed ex leader del partito neonazista greco Alba Dorata è uscito dal carcere in anticipo, in libertà condizionale
content: Nikos Michaloliakos, fondatore ed ex leader del partito greco di estrema destra Alba Dorata,  carcere in anticipo in libertà condizionale: è stato rilasciato mercoledì, ma la notizia è stata diffusa giovedì e  all’agenzia di stampa  da una fonte della polizia.Michaloliakos  nell’ottobre del 2020, quando il tribunale di Atene stabilì che Alba Dorata aveva agito come un’organizzazione criminale. Furono condannati anche altri leader del partito, con pene tra i cinque e i sette anni.Alba Dorata era un partito politico greco di estrema destra dalle idee neonaziste e xenofobe. Fu fondato nel novembre del 1993, ma divenne rilevante in particolare dopo la crisi finanziaria del 2008, riuscendo anche a risultare il terzo più votato alle elezioni del 2015 nel paese. Si sciolse nel 2020, proprio in seguito alle condanne. Michaloliakos dovrà comunque risiedere nella regione greca dell’Attica (quella di Atene), dovrà presentarsi alla polizia una volta al mese e non potrà parlare con nessun’altra persona accusata nel processo con il quale fu condannato. L’accusa  che farà ricorso contro la decisione del tribunale di rilasciarlo in anticipo.
en_title: The founder and former leader of the Greek neo-Nazi party Alba Dorata left the prison in advance, in parole
en_content: Nikos Michaloliakos, founder and former leader of the right-wing Greek party Alba Dorata, in advance in parole: was released on Wednesday, but the news was spread on Thursday and the news agency by a police source. Michaloliakos in October 2020, when the Athens court ruled that Golden Dawn had acted as a criminal organization. Other party leaders were also condemned, with penalties between five and seven years. Golden Dawn was a Greek political party of extreme right from the neonazi and xenophobic ideas. It was founded in November 1993, but became relevant in particular after the 2008 financial crisis, also resulting in the third most voted in the 2015 elections in the country. He disbanded in 2020, just after the convictions. Michaloliakos will still have to reside in the Greek region of the Attica (the one in Athens), he will have to present himself to the police once a month and he will not be able to speak with any other person accused in the trial with which he was convicted. The prosecution that will appeal against the court’s decision to release it in advance.
